evals.prompt: I need to add OAuth2 login (Google and GitHub providers) to our existing Express.js REST API. We currently use session-based auth with Passport.js. The API serves a React frontend at app.example.com. Users table is in PostgreSQL with columns: id, email, password_hash, created_at. We need to keep existing email/password login working while adding the OAuth options. The React frontend already has login/signup pages — we just need the backend endpoints and the frontend buttons/callbacks. We use Jest for testing and deploy to AWS ECS via GitHub Actions.
evals.expected_output: A decomposition with 4-7 task cards covering: discovery/scaffolding, backend OAuth endpoints, frontend OAuth buttons, testing, and possibly migration for OAuth fields. Should use vertical slices where possible and identify that the users table needs new columns.

assertions.text: Output is written as a TASKS/ directory containing MANIFEST.md and individual TASK-NN.md files
Each TASK-NN.md includes an Execution Protocol section and a Guardrails section before the Task Card
MANIFEST.md includes Per-Task Checklist and Wave Merge Protocol sections
MANIFEST.md contains a Decomposition Summary section with Goal, Mode, Affected Areas, Workstreams, Risk, Discovered Facts, Decision Ledger (4 buckets), Coverage Map, and Execution Waves
Decomposition Summary includes a Mode field set to one of: standard_decomposition, brownfield_extension, gap_closure, migration_heavy
Tasks are organized into execution waves with clear sequencing and dependency references
MANIFEST.md includes a Decision Ledger with Locked Decisions, Coding Agent's Discretion, Deferred / Out of Scope, and Open Questions buckets
MANIFEST.md includes a Coverage Map with Source Requirements / Behaviors mapped to task IDs, plus Unmapped Items and Overloaded Tasks sections

Every task card includes: Goal, Addresses, Context Anchor (with Why, Required Reading, Predecessor Outputs, Patterns to Follow), In Scope, Non-Goals, Dependencies, Locked Decisions, Must-Haves (with Truths, Artifacts, Key Links), Acceptance Criteria, Verification Commands, Change Safety, Failure Signals, and Checkpoint
Each task card has a single primary purpose
Cards do not fabricate exact filenames or paths that were not provided in the prompt
Must-Haves Truths entries describe observable behavior, not implementation steps — no vague phrases like 'works correctly'
Database schema changes are in a separate card from application logic
MANIFEST.md includes a Follow-up Tasks section
MANIFEST.md contains a Discovered Facts section with verified repo evidence or notes that repo was not available for verification
In Scope fields reference concrete paths or explicitly label unknowns as estimated
Discovery cards are absent or, if present, explicitly state why upfront discovery could not resolve the unknown
If discovery cards exist, they specify writing outputs to a DISCOVERY-NN.md file, and dependent cards reference that file as required reading
Tasks that create files include at least one Artifacts entry with a substance constraint (min_lines, contains, or exports)
Tasks with visual/UX output have checkpoint type human-verify; most other tasks have checkpoint type None
Every task card includes a Change Safety field classifying the change as additive, reversible, feature-flagged, coordinated, cleanup-later, or unknown
Every task card includes a Failure Signals field identifying signs of underspecification or regression risk
Every task card includes an Acceptance Criteria section with specific, testable criteria
Every task card includes an Addresses field linking to requirements, behaviors, or failed truths
MANIFEST.md includes a Checker Summary section listing dimensions checked and any revisions made
Verification commands in each task are executable after the task's wave completes — no commands depend on future-wave outputs

Every type, interface, or artifact referenced in a task's Must-Haves or In Scope is either produced by the task itself, defined in a predecessor task's Artifacts, or exists in the repo — no task references artifacts that trace to nothing
No task's Patterns to Follow or Predecessor Outputs reference files created by a same-wave parallel task
Every implementation task's primary artifacts map to at least one test task somewhere in the plan — no production artifact is left entirely untested
No single task creates more than ~12 new files or implements more than ~5 independent handlers/controllers — tasks that exceed these heuristics are split further
Authentication and authorization requirements appear as Truths in Must-Haves for tasks that create auth-gated endpoints, not buried only in Constraints or Notes

evals.prompt: We need to migrate our primary users table from a single MySQL database to PostgreSQL. The table has 12 million rows and is read/written by 3 services: the main API (Node.js), a background job processor (Python/Celery), and an admin dashboard (Rails). We need zero downtime during the migration. The table has foreign key relationships with orders, sessions, and audit_logs tables. We're on AWS and currently use RDS MySQL 8.0. Target is RDS PostgreSQL 15. We have no existing PostgreSQL infrastructure.
evals.expected_output: A decomposition with 8-12 task cards covering: discovery, PostgreSQL infrastructure setup, schema creation, dual-write implementation across all 3 services, data backfill/sync, read cutover, write cutover, cleanup.

evals.prompt: We're adding multi-tenancy to our SaaS analytics platform. Currently it's single-tenant — one deployment per customer on separate subdomains (acme.analytics.io, globex.analytics.io). We want to move to a shared infrastructure model where all tenants share the same database and application instances but data is isolated. The stack is: Next.js frontend, FastAPI backend, PostgreSQL database, Redis for caching, and Celery for async jobs. We have about 40 tables, ~200 API endpoints, and 15 background job types. Current customer count is 23 but we're targeting 500+ in the next year. We use Alembic for migrations, pytest for backend tests, and Cypress for e2e.
evals.expected_output: A complex decomposition with 10-15+ task cards. Should include discovery, tenant model, data isolation, API middleware, cache isolation, job tenant awareness, migration, testing, and staged rollout.

evals.prompt: Build a REST API for a task management system from scratch. Stack: Django 5, Django REST Framework, PostgreSQL, pytest. Features: users can create projects, add tasks to projects with title/description/status/priority/due date, assign tasks to project members, and filter/sort tasks by any field. Auth via Django's built-in auth with JWT tokens (djangorestframework-simplejwt). Need full CRUD on all resources, proper permissions (project owners can manage members, members can manage tasks), and pagination. Include a docker-compose.yml for local dev with PostgreSQL. This is a brand new repo — no existing code.
evals.expected_output: A greenfield decomposition with 5-8 task cards using standard_decomposition mode. Should include: project scaffold with Docker, domain models and migrations, auth/JWT setup, API endpoints (likely vertical by resource or horizontal by layer), permissions, and tests. Early waves should be horizontal (scaffold, models) before transitioning to vertical feature delivery.
